grammarReference.structure: Gerunds and infinitives: enjoy watching, want to see
grammarReference.explanation: After enjoy, like and love, use verb + -ing: I enjoy watching cartoons. After want, would like and need, use to + verb: I want to see a film. Do not mix them: enjoy to watch (×) → enjoy watching (✓).
examples: Minh enjoys watching comedy programmes on television.
Linh wants to see a new film at the cinema.
We love listening to music on the radio.
Do you want to watch the news tonight?
commonMistakes: I enjoy to watch cartoons (×) → I enjoy watching cartoons (✓)
She wants seeing a film (×) → She wants to see a film (✓)
They enjoy watch television (×) → They enjoy watching television (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Use too + adjective to say something is more than you want: The screen is too small. Use adjective + enough to say something is sufficient: The room is dark enough for a film. Enough can also come before nouns: We have enough time.
examples: The comedy was too boring for Minh.
The film is exciting enough for the whole family.
The television screen is too small for a big audience.
We have enough time to watch one programme.
commonMistakes: The screen is enough small (×) → The screen is too small (✓)
It is too enough exciting (×) → It is exciting enough (✓)
The programme is too boring enough (×) → The programme is too boring (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Combine enjoy/want patterns with too/enough when giving opinions about media. Enjoy + -ing for activities you like; want to + verb for plans; too + adjective for problems; adjective + enough for satisfaction.
examples: Minh enjoys watching cartoons but the news is too boring.
Linh wants to see the film because the actor is interesting enough.
The documentary is interesting enough — I enjoy learning from it.
The screen is too small, so we want to watch at the cinema.
commonMistakes: I enjoy to watch but it is too boring (×) → I enjoy watching but it is too boring (✓)
She wants see a film enough exciting (×) → She wants to see a film exciting enough (✓)
The comedy is too enough funny (×) → The comedy is funny enough (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Complete Conversations with Missing Sentences
lessons.learningObjective: Choose the best missing sentence to complete a TV conversation.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the TV Conversation
exercises.instructions: Read the passage. Choose the best missing sentence.

passage.title: Minh and Linh Choose a Programme
passage.text:
Minh is eleven and lives in Hanoi. After homework he often watches television with his friend Linh. Tonight they cannot agree on a programme.

Minh: "Let's watch the news on channel three."
Linh: "The news is too boring! I want to see a cartoon."
Minh: "Cartoons are for little children. I enjoy watching documentaries."
Linh: "Documentaries can be interesting, but your screen is too small. Let's watch a comedy — comedies are my favourite programmes."
Minh: "OK, but only if the actor is funny enough. Last week's comedy was boring."
Linh: "This one has an exciting scene on a ship. The whole audience at school said it was interesting."

They changed the channel and found a comedy with a famous actor. Minh stopped the programme once because the screen was too dark, but Linh said the film was exciting enough. In the end both friends enjoyed watching together.

passage.title: Minh and Linh Choose a Programme
passage.text:
Minh is eleven and lives in Hanoi. After homework he often watches television with his friend Linh. Tonight they cannot agree on a programme.

Minh: "Let's watch the news on channel three."
Linh: "The news is too boring! I want to see a cartoon."
Minh: "Cartoons are for little children. I enjoy watching documentaries."
Linh: "Documentaries can be interesting, but your screen is too small. Let's watch a comedy — comedies are my favourite programmes."
Minh: "OK, but only if the actor is funny enough. Last week's comedy was boring."
Linh: "This one has an exciting scene on a ship. The whole audience at school said it was interesting."

They changed the channel and found a comedy with a famous actor. Minh stopped the programme once because the screen was too dark, but Linh said the film was exciting enough. In the end both friends enjoyed watching together.

questions.explanation: Programme — họ không đồng ý chương trình xem.

questions.questionText: Linh thinks comedies are her _____ programmes.
questions.explanation: Favourite — comedies are my favourite programmes.

script.title: Choosing What to Watch

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: There are too many programmes on television tonight!

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: I know! I enjoy watching cartoons, but you think they are boring.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Cartoons are OK, but I want to see a documentary about animals.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Documentaries can be interesting, but the news is too long on this channel.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Let's watch a comedy instead. The actor looks funny enough on the screen.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Yes! Comedies are my favourite. The last video we watched was exciting.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: OK, but if it is too boring, we can change the channel.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Deal! I think the audience online loved this programme too.

script.audioNotes: Friendly TV-choice dialogue between Minh and Linh, opinion-focused. Approx. 55 seconds.

questions.questionText: What does Linh enjoy watching?
questions.explanation: Cartoons — Linh nói enjoy watching cartoons.

questions.questionText: What does Minh think about cartoons?
questions.explanation: OK but he wants a documentary — thái độ trung lập/khác Linh.

choices.text: They are OK but he prefers a documentary
They are too boring for everyone

exercises.instructions: Listen again. Focus on opinions.

script.title: Planning a Cinema Trip

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Minh, do you want to see the new film at the cinema?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Yes! I enjoy watching films on a big screen — television is too small.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: The documentary looks interesting, but I prefer an exciting comedy.

lines.speaker: Dad
lines.text: The comedy starts at two. Is that early enough for you?

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Perfect. The actor in that film is my favourite.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: I heard the best scene is on a ship. The audience clapped loudly!

lines.speaker: Dad
lines.text: We can listen to the radio in the car. The news said the film is popular.

lines.speaker: Minh
lines.text: Great! Watching at the cinema is much better when the programme is exciting enough.

script.audioNotes: Family cinema planning with opinions and attitudes. Warm tone. Approx. 50 seconds.

questions.explanation: Yes + let's choose comedy — từ listening script.

choices.text: Yes, there are too many programmes! Let's watch a comedy instead.

exercises.title: Check: Talk About Media Opinions
exercises.instructions: Give your opinions about TV and films. Speak for up to 120 seconds.

content.prompt: Tell the examiner what you enjoy watching and what is too boring.
content.sceneDescription: Timeline: after homework watch TV → too many programmes → enjoy cartoons/documentaries/comedy → news too long → cinema screen big enough → radio in kitchen.
followUpQuestions: What do you enjoy watching on television?
What programme is too boring for you?
Do you want to see films at the cinema? Why?
What makes a documentary interesting enough?
Who listens to the radio in Minh's family?
suggestedAnswers: I enjoy watching comedies and documentaries.
The news is sometimes too boring.
Yes, because the screen is big enough.
When it is about animals or Hanoi.
Grandma listens to the radio in the kitchen.

assessmentCriteria.pronunciation: Media words (programme, documentary, audience) are clear enough to understand.
assessmentCriteria.fluency: Answers are connected; brief pauses are acceptable at Flyers level.
assessmentCriteria.grammar: Uses enjoy + -ing, want to + verb, and too/enough in short phrases.
assessmentCriteria.vocabulary: Uses media and entertainment vocabulary from the unit.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Best Opinion Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best spoken response.

questions.questionText: Best way to describe all three media:
questions.explanation: TV + radio + cinema — review toàn unit.

choices.text: Television radio cinema enjoy film exciting.
I enjoy watching television after school. Grandma listens to the radio, and I want to see exciting films at the cinema.
I enjoy to watch radio cinema enough.

questions.questionText: Best answer: What makes a programme interesting?
questions.explanation: Good actor + exciting scene — chi tiết cụ thể.

choices.text: A programme is interesting when the actor is good and there is an exciting scene.

exercises.title: Review: Media Opinion Speaking Mix
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say.

questions.questionText: Best summary of Minh and Linh's TV habits:
questions.explanation: Different tastes + enjoy together — tóm tắt unit.

choices.text: Minh Linh documentary comedy enjoy together.
Minh enjoys watching documentaries and Linh loves comedies, but they enjoy watching together in the end.

questions.questionText: Best closing answer in a speaking test:
questions.explanation: Favourite + exciting enough — kết thúc tự nhiên.

choices.text: My favourite programme is a comedy because it is exciting enough and never too boring.
Favourite is enjoy to watch programme.
